description: This figure illustrates the geometric dimensions of two standardized mechanical test specimens for Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steel property characterization. The first subplot shows a cylindrical tensile specimen with M12 threaded ends: it has a gauge section with 5 mm diameter and 30 mm length, R5 transition fillets, 13 mm long end clamping sections, 65 mm total length, and a 60° central positioning hole at each end. The second subplot presents a standard V-notched Charpy impact specimen with dimensions of 10 mm × 10 mm × 55 mm, and a 2 mm deep V-notch. These specimens are applied for room temperature tensile tests at a strain rate of 1.1×10^-3 s^-1 and -40℃ Charpy impact tests respectively, providing unified specimen specifications to guarantee repeatability and comparability of tested mechanical property results.
